supplementEntry.name: Bovine Colostrum
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Multiple systematic reviews and meta-analyses (Jones 2016, Hajihashemi 2024, Li 2019) plus 10+ individual RCTs across immune function, gut permeability, and GI health. Strongest evidence for reducing upper respiratory infections (5 RCTs pooled) and gut permeability (10 RCTs pooled in meta-analysis).

cognitive: Limited direct evidence for cognitive benefits

mechanismsOfAction: Immunoglobulin (IgG, IgA) transfer providing passive immunity
Growth factor delivery (IGF-1, TGF-beta, EGF) supporting gut epithelial repair
Tight junction protein support reducing intestinal permeability
Inhibition of epithelial cell apoptosis under stress

contraindications: Cow's milk allergy or lactose intolerance
drugInteractions: May theoretically interact with immunosuppressive medications due to immune-modulating properties

effectSizes.leanBodyMass: +1.49 kg over 8 weeks at 20g/day -- Antonio 2001 RCT
effectSizes.urtiSymptomFrequencyChildren: 31% median reduction over 20 weeks -- Halasa 2023 RCT

keyCitations.authors: Jones AW, March DS, Curtis F, Bridle C
keyCitations.title: Bovine colostrum supplementation and upper respiratory symptoms during exercise training: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials
keyCitations.journal: BMC Sports Science, Medicine and Rehabilitation

keyCitations.authors: Hajihashemi P, Haghighatdoost F, Kassaian N, et al.
keyCitations.title: Bovine Colostrum in Increased Intestinal Permeability in Healthy Athletes and Patients: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Clinical Trials

keyCitations.authors: Li J, Xu YW, Jiang JJ, Song QK
keyCitations.title: Bovine colostrum and product intervention associated with relief of childhood infectious diarrhea

keyCitations.authors: Marchbank T, Davison G, Oakes JR, et al.
keyCitations.title: The nutriceutical bovine colostrum truncates the increase in gut permeability caused by heavy exercise in athletes
keyCitations.journal: American Journal of Physiology - Gastrointestinal and Liver Physiology

mechanisms.tissueTarget: Mucosal surfaces and systemic immune system
mechanisms.target: Mucosal surfaces and systemic immune system

evidence.title: Immunological Outcomes of Bovine Colostrum Supplementation in Trained and Physically Active People: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
evidence.findings: Reviewed 10 RCTs (239 participants). No or fairly low impact on serum immunoglobulins (IgA, IgG), lymphocytes, neutrophils, and salivary IgA in athletes, suggesting colostrum's immune benefits may act through mucosal rather than systemic pathways.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 10 RCTs in trained and physically active people

evidence.title: The effects of bovine colostrum supplementation on in vivo immunity following prolonged exercise: a randomised controlled trial
evidence.findings: Colostrum enhanced immune sensitivity at 24h (p<0.001) and 48h (p=0.023) post-exercise; blunted exercise-induced decrease in immune responsiveness in 31 men.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT measuring in vivo immunity following prolonged exercise

mechanisms.mechanism: Growth factor delivery supporting gut epithelial repair

evidence.title: The nutriceutical bovine colostrum truncates the increase in gut permeability caused by heavy exercise in athletes
evidence.journal: American Journal of Physiology - Gastrointestinal and Liver Physiology
evidence.findings: Colostrum truncated exercise-induced gut permeability rise by 80% (placebo saw 2.5-fold increase). Truncated temperature-induced apoptosis by 60% in cell lines, indicating growth factor-mediated epithelial protection.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled, crossover RCT in 12 volunteers with cell line mechanistic studies

evidence.title: Co-administration of the health food supplement, bovine colostrum, reduces the acute non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug-induced increase in intestinal permeability
evidence.findings: Colostrum co-administration prevented the 3-fold increase in gut permeability typically induced by indomethacin in 7 healthy volunteers and 15 chronic NSAID patients.
evidence.methodology: Randomized controlled crossover trial in healthy volunteers and chronic NSAID patients

mechanisms.mechanism: Tight junction protein support reducing intestinal permeability

evidence.title: Oral Supplementation with Bovine Colostrum Decreases Intestinal Permeability and Stool Concentrations of Zonulin in Athletes
evidence.findings: After 20 days of 500mg colostrum, intestinal permeability returned to normal range. Significant reduction in stool zonulin concentrations vs placebo, indicating improved tight junction integrity.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled, crossover RCT in 16 athletes

evidence.title: Effects of early enteral bovine colostrum supplementation on intestinal permeability in critically ill patients: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study
evidence.findings: Plasma endotoxin decreased significantly by day 10; zonulin levels reduced significantly (p<0.001); diarrhea incidence significantly lower (p=0.02) in 62 ICU patients.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT in 70 ICU patients (62 completed)

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces upper respiratory symptom days and episodes during exercise training
benefits.replicationStatus: Replicated across 5 RCTs with meta-analytic confirmation

evidence.title: Bovine colostrum supplementation and upper respiratory symptoms during exercise training: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials
evidence.journal: BMC Sports Science, Medicine and Rehabilitation
evidence.findings: Bovine colostrum reduced upper respiratory symptom days by 44% (rate ratio 0.56, 95% CI 0.43-0.72) and episodes by 38% (rate ratio 0.62, 95% CI 0.40-0.99) over 8-12 weeks.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 5 RCTs

evidence.title: Effects of bovine colostrum supplementation on upper respiratory illness in active males
evidence.findings: Significantly lower proportion of URI days and episodes with colostrum vs placebo over 12 weeks. Mechanism: colostrum limits increased salivary bacterial load.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT with 25 colostrum vs 28 placebo

evidence.title: Moderate Dose Bovine Colostrum Supplementation in Prevention of Upper Respiratory Tract Infections in Medical University Students: A Randomized, Triple Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial
evidence.findings: Significant protection from URTIs with 0.5-1.0 g/day, reduced frequency and severity of symptoms in medical/health science students.
evidence.methodology: Randomized, triple-blind, placebo-controlled trial

evidence.title: Six Weeks of Supplementation with Bovine Colostrum Effectively Reduces URTIs Symptoms Frequency and Gravity for Up to 20 Weeks in Pre-School Children
evidence.findings: 31% reduction in URTI symptom frequency (significant to 20 weeks), 37% reduction in symptom gravity, 50% reduction in severe episodes. No significant side effects.
evidence.methodology: RCT in pre-school children

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces intestinal permeability in athletes and patients
benefits.replicationStatus: Replicated across 10 RCTs with meta-analytic confirmation

evidence.title: Bovine Colostrum in Increased Intestinal Permeability in Healthy Athletes and Patients: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Clinical Trials
evidence.findings: Significant reduction in 5-h urinary lactulose/rhamnose ratio and lactulose/mannitol ratio, indicating reduced intestinal permeability across athletes and patients.
evidence.methodology: Meta-analysis of 10 randomized clinical trials

evidence.title: The nutriceutical bovine colostrum truncates the increase in gut permeability caused by heavy exercise in athletes
evidence.journal: American Journal of Physiology - Gastrointestinal and Liver Physiology
evidence.findings: Colostrum truncated exercise-induced gut permeability rise by 80% compared to placebo (which saw 2.5-fold increase).
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled, crossover RCT

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces childhood infectious diarrhea frequency and occurrence

evidence.title: Bovine colostrum and product intervention associated with relief of childhood infectious diarrhea
evidence.findings: Stool frequency reduced by 1.42 times/day; diarrhea occurrence reduced by 71% (OR 0.29).
evidence.methodology: Meta-analysis of 5 RCTs on childhood infectious diarrhea

evidence.title: Bovine Colostrum in the Treatment of Acute Diarrhea in Children: A Double-Blinded Randomized Controlled Trial
evidence.findings: At 48h, significantly lower frequency of vomiting, diarrhea, and Vesikari scoring vs placebo.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, randomized controlled trial in children with acute diarrhea

benefits.specificClaim: Improves lean body mass and sprint performance in active individuals

evidence.title: The effects of bovine colostrum supplementation on body composition and exercise performance in active men and women
evidence.findings: Significant increase in bone-free lean body mass (mean +1.49 kg, p<0.05) with 20g/day colostrum vs whey protein. No improvement in treadmill time or 1RM strength.
evidence.methodology: RCT comparing 20g/day colostrum vs whey protein over 8 weeks

evidence.title: The effect of bovine colostrum supplementation on exercise performance in elite field hockey players
evidence.journal: International Journal of Sport Nutrition and Exercise Metabolism
evidence.findings: Sprint performance improved significantly (p=0.023) with colostrum vs whey. No differences in body composition or endurance.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled RCT in elite athletes

safety.safetyAspect: General tolerability and clinical safety profile
safety.claim: Good safety profile across multiple populations including children, adults, athletes, and ICU patients

evidence.title: Clinical applications of bovine colostrum therapy: a systematic review
evidence.findings: Systematic review of 51 eligible studies concluded colostrum is nontoxic and safe for human ingestion. Colostrum may provide gastrointestinal and immunological benefits across diverse populations.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of 51 studies evaluating clinical applications

evidence.title: Bovine Colostrum Applications in Sick and Healthy People: A Systematic Review
evidence.findings: Reviewed 28 papers finding colostrum effective for preventing upper respiratory illness in sportsmen, modulating immune response, and reducing intestinal permeability with good safety across sick and healthy populations.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of 28 papers across sick and healthy populations

evidence.title: Six Weeks of Supplementation with Bovine Colostrum Effectively Reduces URTIs Symptoms Frequency and Gravity for Up to 20 Weeks in Pre-School Children
evidence.findings: No significant side effects reported in pre-school children over 6 weeks of supplementation with 20-week follow-up.
evidence.methodology: RCT in 57 pre-school children

dosage.claim: Dose range established across multiple clinical trials with lower doses effective for URTI prevention and higher doses for body composition

evidence.title: Moderate Dose Bovine Colostrum Supplementation in Prevention of Upper Respiratory Tract Infections in Medical University Students: A Randomized, Triple Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial
evidence.findings: Significant URTI protection with moderate doses of 0.5-1.0 g/day over 107 days, demonstrating efficacy at lower doses than traditionally used.
evidence.methodology: Randomized, triple-blind, placebo-controlled trial in medical university students
